title: UC Quality Profile — Gold / Silver / Bronze Tiers
description: Overlay schema defining tiered quality requirements for use cases. Quality means operational utility: can someone implement this UC end-to-end from this page alone? The base schema (uc.schema.json) remains the structural contract; this profile defines depth expectations.
x-philosophy: Quality is operational utility, not field-count compliance. Fewer excellent UCs beat many shallow ones. The audit measures depth, not just presence.

bronze.description: Bronze — understand and start investigating. The UC has enough metadata and SPL to be useful, with a short implementation note.

silver.description: Silver — implement with some external reference. Core fields plus an implementation guide with at least prerequisites, configuration, and validation. At least one reference.

detailedImplementation.x-depth-requirement: Must contain at least 3 substantive sections (e.g. Prerequisites, Configure, Validate). Sections must contain product-specific detail, not generic boilerplate.

gold.description: Gold — implement from this page alone. Full 5-step implementation guide with product-specific depth: API paths, input names, field names, vendor UI comparison, RBAC roles, failure modes. The quality test: could an engineer who has never used this product implement this UC end-to-end without leaving this page?

description.x-quality-note: Explains what the UC detects or monitors. Must be distinct from value.

value.x-quality-note: Explains why this UC matters. Must be distinct from description — description says 'what', value says 'why it matters'.

dataSources.x-quality-note: Must name the index, sourcetype, and collection method/API path.

detailedImplementation.x-depth-requirement: Must contain 5 named sections with product-specific detail: (1) Prerequisites — versions, licenses, roles, network access. (2) Configure data collection — specific input names, API paths, poll intervals, expected event volume. (3) Create the search — SPL with 'Understanding this SPL' explanation. (4) Validate — how to verify data is correct and complete (compare to vendor UI, count checks, field spot-checks). (5) Operationalize and Troubleshoot — dashboard layout, time ranges, access control, runbook links, specific failure modes and their causes.

references.x-quality-note: At least Splunkbase link + vendor documentation.

x-depth-checks.description-value-distinct: description and value must have Levenshtein distance > 0.3 (normalized). Anti-pattern: UC-5.1.4 copies description into value verbatim.
x-depth-checks.implementation-substance: detailedImplementation must contain product-specific terms (sourcetype names, API paths, field names, input names), not just generic Splunk terminology. A section that says 'Install the TA and enable the input' without naming which input fails this check.
x-depth-checks.validation-vendor-reference: At least one validation step should reference a vendor UI or external comparison point (e.g. 'compare to Catalyst Center > Assurance').
x-depth-checks.troubleshooting-specificity: Troubleshooting must mention at least one product-specific failure mode (not just 'check splunkd.log').
x-depth-checks.consolidation-awareness: UCs within the same subcategory with >80% similarity in SPL or description are consolidation candidates — fewer, deeper UCs are preferred.
